entries.mem0ai/mem0: An official Hermes Agent memory provider offering a universal memory layer for user, session, and agent state. It supports hybrid retrieval via semantic, keyword, and entity matching with flexible deployment options across cloud or self-hosted environments.
entries.supermemoryai/supermemory: An official Hermes Agent memory provider featuring a custom vector graph engine and sub-100ms recall. It specializes in automated fact extraction and integrates with external sources like Google Drive, Notion, and GitHub.
entries.volcengine/OpenViking: An official Hermes Agent memory provider that utilizes a filesystem-based organization and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading. This approach reduces token consumption by up to 91% and provides visualized retrieval trajectories for debugging.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: An opinionated brain for Hermes Agent that synthesizes cited answers and performs explicit gap analysis on missing information. It utilizes a self-wiring knowledge graph and supports scoped multi-user access for institutional memory.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: A learning-focused memory system that implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent growth. It integrates into existing agents as a simple LLM wrapper supporting OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: A reasoning-first memory library that extracts conclusions from conversations and models users and ideas. It combines BM25 and vector retrieval and serves as the upstream library for the honcho-self-hosted wrapper.
entries.campfirein/byterover-cli: An official Hermes Agent memory provider that treats memory as a git repository using markdown context trees. It features 5-tier retrieval and git-like versioning for branching and merging context.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: A recursive self-improving context harness that uses real-world evaluation and feedback loops to enhance agent performance. It generates persistent playbooks and structured execution traces across providers like Claude Code and Pi.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system featuring sub-millisecond retrieval latency via SQLite storage. It supports MCP for seamless integration and utilizes temporal triples and episodic compression for advanced management.
entries.stephenschoenler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG to compact conversation history without losing detail. It persists raw messages in SQLite and provides tools for grepping and expanding summaries.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: A self-hosted backend for Honcho that enables private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It supports local inference servers and OpenAI-compatible providers via a Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis setup.
entries.Signet-AI/signetai: A local-first identity and secrets layer that provides portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and MCP clients. It uses SQLite for storage and includes a repairable system for editing or reclassifying context.
entries.plur-ai/plur: A shared memory layer using an open YAML engram format to eliminate API costs in multi-agent systems. It employs ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts while decaying stale information.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: A local-first on-device context engine that operates without cloud dependencies or API keys. It combines BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs, integrating via MCP servers and Claude Code hooks.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: A context optimization tool that increases prefill speed by 1.5–3× through prefix cache reuse. It reduces prompt token usage by approximately 36% and provides native plugin support for Hermes Agent.
entries.yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin: An embedded, self-maintaining plugin that provides explainable recall using 'why_retrieved' tags. It features contradiction tracking and canonicalization without requiring a separate server or GPU.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: A memory kernel stack that consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval. It leverages a multi-layered storage approach using SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma to reduce prompt noise.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: A reflective memory system combining BM25 full-text search with ranked graph traversal. It automatically indexes local directories, URLs, and multimedia files using edge tags for navigable relationships.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: An anticipatory memory system that prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs. It uses a local-first architecture with SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: A utility that reduces prompt token overhead by using local BM25 ranking for tool selection. It includes a dashboard to track schema token savings and is compatible with Hermes Agent v0.14.0.
entries.Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin: A local-only plugin using an embedded .lbdb graph database with importance-weighted recall. It surfaces high-priority memories first and supports optional GLiNER2 entity extraction.
entries.MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory: A pull-model episodic memory plugin that ensures complete deletion of memory events to prevent outdated summaries from affecting recall. It provides a trace.jsonl audit log for all memory operations.
entries.futurebrowser/hermes-exabase-plugin: A plugin that integrates Exabase M-1 for self-organizing long-term memory. It provides tools for manual storage and searching, along with configurable query expansion and reranking.

entries.nexu-io/open-design: A local-first open-source design and prototyping system that supports Hermes Agent via a coding-agent CLI. It enables the generation of prototypes, motion graphics, and decks using a vast library of design systems and plugins.
entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: A collection of 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like NIST CSF 2.0 and MITRE ATT&K. These skills use the agentskills.io standard to bring specialized security domain expertise to agentic workflows.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill converts natural language and codebases into .drawio XML diagrams. It supports various presets including UML, ERD, and Architecture, utilizing over 10,000 official shapes and AI brand logos.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: A content auditing tool that eliminates detectable AI writing patterns through rewrite, detect, and edit-in-place modes. It is compatible with Hermes and other AI editors, utilizing a tiered word replacement table and 48 pattern categories.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: An agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across teams and devices with native integration for Hermes and OpenAI-compatible APIs.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library providing frameworks for UX, marketing, and software architecture. These skills are compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io agents, installable via CLI or marketplace.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically propose and generate reusable skills. It produces both Python plugin scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation to capture recurring patterns.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: Provides type-safe support for the agentskills.io specification within Pydantic AI. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce token usage and supports both programmatic and filesystem-based definitions.
entries.ZeroPointRepo/youtube-skills: A set of skills for extracting transcripts, captions, and timestamps from YouTube without needing yt-dlp. It enables Hermes Agent and other compatible tools to browse channels and extract playlists.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community hub for browsing and installing skills featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes a creator marketplace with Stripe and crypto payments and an agent-to-agent reliability scoring system.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: These skills teach Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions. It helps agents identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on analysis blind spots.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: A harness for long-running agents that provides structured memory and a plan-approve-execute lifecycle. It stores work and proof in plain files and offers integration skills for Codex and Claude Code agents.
entries.AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow: A workflow that transforms rough ideas into structured design documents and implementation handoffs. It offers Lite and Full modes and includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: A literate programming skill that weaves code and documentation into .lit.md files. It uses a tangler to extract runnable source files and automatically syncs edits back to the literate document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: A self-memory system that captures high-value decisions and outcomes via shared markdown files. It provides a pipeline for fine-tuning and switching to replacement models based on captured work.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Implements the agentskills.io spec to enable agent interaction with the Chainlink oracle network. It supports integrations for CCIP, VRF, and Data Feeds, installable via the Vercel skills CLI.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Converts documentation, repositories, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills. It prevents instruction rot by tracing instructions to specific file lines and pinned commits.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: Official skills for FLUX image generation providing standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2. It includes API integration patterns for webhooks and polling, supporting precise hex color specifications.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A registry of reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It features an OpenAPI-driven SDK for versioned installation with recursive dependency resolution and commerce integration via Stripe and TON.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: Enables autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with real-time progress tracking. It includes built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery during long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token consumption. It features a socket-based daemon for persistent connections and supports hot-reloading of configurations.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: A Hermes Agent skill that manages Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: A C# / .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with MCP integration. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool loading.
entries.Sahil-SS9/hermes-multichannel-prompt-opt: A plugin that rewrites and scores prompts across various surfaces like Discord, Telegram, and CLI. It reduces token usage by 20–80% by tailoring prompt structures to specific model capabilities.
entries.willingning-coder/eagle-eye: An intelligent retrieval system using a 5-layer approach including FTS5, dense embeddings, and RRF fusion. It filters large skill libraries down to the five most relevant skills to minimize token consumption.
entries.mag3nt-com/openclaw-skill: Automates payments for HTTP 402 responses using USDC via protocols like Pay Link and x402. It is compatible with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and the agentskills.io standard.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides Nix packages for AI coding agents with daily automated updates for both source-based and binary distributions. It allows users to manage Hermes alongside other major agents like Claude Code and Goose within a reproducible Nix environment.
entries.marshallrichards/ClawPhone: ClawPhone offers automated installers and configuration guides for running agent CLIs on ARM64 Android devices via Termux. It specifically enables hardware and UI access through Termux:API and Termux:GUI, allowing Hermes to interact directly with smartphone features.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker or complex setups. It features a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine, supporting local model execution via LM Studio and portable AI generation servers.
entries.AbuZar-Ansarii/Hermes-Agent-On-Android: This repository features a one-line installation script designed to deploy Hermes Agent within Android Termux environments. It prioritizes privacy and cost reduction by supporting local execution and compatibility with over 200 different models.
entries.ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart: This community Helm chart enables cloud-native Hermes deployment on Kubernetes with a focus on state-safety guardrails. It enforces single-writer persistent storage and offers configurable gateway modes with API and webhook listeners for production workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw provides a highly secure sandbox for Hermes Agent using hardware-level enforcement for network, filesystem, and process limits. It includes dynamic security policy presets and a pre-built library of over 40 integrated tools and skills.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific versions of Hermes using build arguments.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services. It focuses on security through sops-nix integration for secrets handling and supports a hybrid model of Nix-managed and interactive CLI skills.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Designed for production-ready headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian, this tool configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integrates with the Nous Portal.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple Hermes Agent instances with distinct workspaces and identities. It facilitates enterprise-grade operations through hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation and MCP access for external AI clients.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack integrated with the Portainer UI and a ttyd web terminal for remote access. It simplifies management by deploying as a Portainer Git Repository stack with automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration stack that manages multi-agent workflows across CrewAI, LangGraph, and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It ensures output quality through the integrated Aegis review system and provides detailed spend monitoring.
entries.agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires: A TUI and PWA dashboard designed for managing diverse coding agents like Hermes and Claude Code in parallel. It utilizes git worktrees and container sandboxing via Docker or Podman to ensure isolated execution across multi-repo workspaces.
entries.CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room: A governance-focused template that provides a sidecar control plane for managing Hermes agents. It employs task bus patterns to facilitate structured delegation between orchestrators and specialist teams.
entries.Agent-3-7/minions: A supervision interface for Hermes agents featuring a Kanban board for tracking autonomous task status. It emphasizes human-in-the-loop verification with live streaming of agent reasoning and tool execution.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: A framework for building autonomous swarms that supports over 23 LLM providers, including Hermes and Ollama. It features durable memory and a unique system for learning new skills from reviewed conversations.
entries.shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room: A scaling template that uses a level-based architecture to transition from single VPS agents to automated specialist teams. It includes bundled skills for security auditing, backup management, and VPS provisioning.
entries.linke-ai/hermes-agent-team: A local collaboration system that orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents using MCP and ACP protocols. It provides a real-time Web UI to monitor agent dialogues and Kanban-based task lifecycles.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized collective of 17 agents dedicated to research, planning, and implementation. The framework enforces strict quality gates via dedicated reviewer and tester agents with priority-based conflict resolution.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization harness that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine agent code. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track and improve performance frontiers.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It orchestrates nine agents using Paperclip and Hermes Agent, visualizing the competition via a real-time scoreboard and task dashboard.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: An ACP-style delegation system that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It prevents parent context pollution through isolated workflows and enforces safety via output limits and timeouts.

entries.colbymchenry/codegraph: This tool optimizes Hermes Agent performance by providing a local, pre-indexed knowledge graph of symbol relationships and call graphs. It significantly reduces token consumption and tool calls by up to 58%, offering a high-efficiency alternative to raw file scanning across macOS, Linux, and Windows.
entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A high-performance Rust-based TUI designed to monitor and visualize token consumption across various AI coding agents including Hermes. It provides developers with a real-time dashboard to track costs and usage patterns directly from the terminal.
entries.liaohch3/claude-tap: This local trace viewer allows developers to inspect the raw API traffic of Hermes Agent, including prompts, tool calls, and streaming responses. It features structured diffs for comparing adjacent requests, making it an essential tool for debugging agent logic and communication.
entries.Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes: An opinionated workflow layer that transforms Hermes into an autonomous CTO capable of handling issue triage, implementation, and QA. It comes pre-packaged with 23 curated skills and deep integrations for platforms like Vercel, Supabase, and GitHub.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal interface with diverse themes ranging from retro DOS to cyberpunk. It supports ASCII art banners, custom spinner verbs, and inherited configurations for easy aesthetic modifications.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A native port of the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills into the Claude Code Channel environment. It eliminates the need for OAuth or external processes while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM inference and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It enables Hermes to navigate the web with human-in-the-loop supervision, featuring durable state management and agent-meaningful bookmarks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific folders or project directories. It allows for seamless switching between global and local agents, each with its own isolated memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman: This setup leverages RTK and Caveman templating to achieve up to 99% token savings during agent operations. It includes over 40 pre-configured skills for finance and DevOps, optimized for automated cross-platform deployment.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A rapid deployment stack that uses Docker to bundle Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local setup. It includes 29 community plugins for memory and validation, pre-configured for local GPU inference.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that uses deterministic detectors to scan Hermes system prompts and tool descriptions for common failure modes. It provides fast, LLM-free scoring and CI/CD integration to ensure agent configurations meet structural quality standards.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A dedicated migration utility that facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automatically maps personas, memories, and complex model configurations into the Hermes YAML format while securely handling credential transfers.

entries.farion1231/cc-switch: A cross-platform manager built with Tauri 2 that provides a unified interface for Hermes Agent and other AI coding tools. It simplifies the management of multiple CLI agents across Windows, macOS, and Linux.
entries.iOfficeAI/AionUi: A local open-source cowork app that provides a GUI for Hermes Agent and over 13 other CLI agents. It features a built-in engine for web search and file access, with the ability to automatically generate editable Word, Excel, and Morph PPT files.
entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A lightweight web-based interface using Python and vanilla JavaScript that maintains full feature parity with the Hermes CLI. It utilizes a three-panel layout featuring a workspace file browser and inline previews for mobile and web access.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app that streamlines the installation and dependency resolution for local or remote Hermes Agent backends. It includes an integrated memory system supporting Mem0 and Honcho, and connects to 16 messaging gateways like Slack and WhatsApp.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A web dashboard focused on session management and channel configuration for platforms including Discord, Telegram, and Matrix. It offers local profile management with scoped memory and skills, alongside an integrated terminal and file browser.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace featuring a Monaco-powered file browser and a PTY terminal. It introduces Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch via tmux-backed workers, complemented by an MCP catalog and skills manager.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that establishes direct SSH connections to Hermes without requiring browser wrappers or daemons. It provides dedicated views for Kanban, cron jobs, and session data for a professional developer experience.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted web dashboard featuring the ZOO Swarm Monitor with a kanban pipeline and live event feed. It provides a comprehensive control plane for MCP servers, including a config editor and real-time log tailing.
entries.jazzyalex/agent-sessions: A local-first macOS session browser and cockpit designed for inspecting transcripts and tracking limits for Hermes and other coding agents. It allows users to resume CLI sessions directly in iTerm2, Warp, or the standard Terminal.
entries.nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1: A native macOS interface specifically designed for Hermes Agent instances running on SSH hosts or Orgo cloud computers. It features one-click automated installation on cloud VMs and integrated WebRTC voice mode via OpenAI Realtime.
entries.Eynzof/hermes-agent-cn-desktop: A Windows-first desktop application built with Rust and Tauri v2, tailored for the Hermes-CN-Core runtime. It includes health checks, update management, and integration with Feishu and various model providers.
entries.liftaris/herm: An alternative TUI and dashboard built with OpenTUI that renders markdown, LaTeX, and tool calls. It uses Kanban boards to monitor agentic work and provides a terminal-based interface for managing profiles and environment configs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed as a Telegram Mini App for local execution. It supports terminal chat with streaming responses, background agent spawning, and system health monitoring via gauges.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no admin rights or Docker. It bundles over 100 tools, including ComfyUI and MusicGen, and includes a dynamic tool maker for creating Python handlers and API wrappers.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A self-hosted, glassmorphic web interface delivered as a single-file React 18 HTML application. It integrates a terminal, file browser, and real-time log streaming for managing AI skills and MCP servers.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A process monitoring dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed with cost tracking and model distribution. It allows for read-only inspection of config files and full-text search across conversation histories.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted AI workspace featuring a chat-first interface with tool approval cards and SSE streaming. It includes a marketplace for discovering MCP servers and skills, with isolated profile management for separate API keys and memory.
entries.Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop: A multi-agent desktop client that orchestrates collaboration between 20 specialized AI agents via a PM orchestrator. It features a visual Skill Store with over 50 one-click installations and supports streaming SSE responses.
